expectations.evidence: The without_skill run was instructed not to read the reference document. The review uses WCAG-style numbering (e.g., '1.1.1', '2.4.1', '2.4.4') rather than KWCAG2.2 sequential 1-33 item numbering. It references 'WCAG 1.1.1' and 'WCAG 2.4.1' instead of KWCAG item numbers, confirming the reference was not consulted.

expectations.text: Evaluates semantic HTML usage (header, nav, main, footer)
expectations.evidence: Section 1.2 '시맨틱 랜드마크 요소 부재 (Critical)' explicitly evaluates all four landmark elements: '<header>: 페이지 헤더 영역이 정의되지 않음', '<nav>: About Us 링크가 네비게이션 역할을 하지만 <nav> 요소로 감싸져 있지 않음', '<main>: 주요 콘텐츠 영역이 <main> 태그로 구분되지 않음', '<footer>: 푸터 영역 없음'. Includes recommended restructured HTML.

expectations.text: Checks ARIA attributes and landmark roles
expectations.evidence: The review mentions aria-live in passing (section 2.4 about innerHTML) but does not systematically check ARIA attributes or landmark roles. There is no evaluation of role attributes, aria-label, aria-labelledby, or other ARIA properties. The review focuses more on semantic HTML elements than ARIA compliance.

expectations.text: Evaluates form input labeling and association
expectations.evidence: The review does not evaluate form input labeling or association at all. There is no mention of <label>, <input>, aria-label for form elements, <fieldset>/<legend>, or any form-related accessibility concerns. While the page has no form elements, the with_skill version explicitly noted this as ✅ 준수 for the relevant items.

expectations.text: Classifies findings using result classification system
expectations.evidence: Uses a severity-based system (Critical/Major/Minor or 심각/주요/경미) and status labels (양호/오류) rather than the KWCAG classification system (✅/❌/⚠️/➖ or O/X/△/N/A). Section 3 organizes findings as '심각 (Critical)', '주요 (Major)', '경미 (Minor)' which is a different classification approach.
